headline: Warsh Era Begins. PCE + GDP Thursday. Markets Pricing Stagflation vs. Earnings Resilience.
sub: Kevin Warsh takes the Fed chair oath this week amid the hottest inflation environment since 2023 — his first public remarks are an instant market mover. The Strait of Hormuz ceasefire holds but shipping remains at just 5% of pre-conflict levels, keeping oil elevated and stagflation risk alive. Thursday delivers the week's binary moment: Q1 GDP (2nd estimate) and core PCE — the Fed's preferred inflation gauge — alongside a dense earnings gauntlet from Salesforce, Costco, and Dell.

events.detail: Thu May 28 — hot PCE could force Warsh's hand on hikes within days of taking office
First remarks watched for rate-hike signal; 4 FOMC dissents preceded him

events.text: Mega Earnings: CRM, COST, DELL, SNOW, MRVL
events.detail: AI capex commentary (Salesforce, Marvell) and consumer health (Costco) — May 27-28
Tue May 26 — first post-Memorial Day data; energy costs and Iran war expected to weigh
Ceasefire in place but traffic at 5% of normal; any incident reverses oil relief rally
Cleveland Fed sees May CPI at 4.2%; FOMC had record 4 dissents — hike probability rising

bias: Tactically cautious with selective upside. Stagflation risk and Warsh policy uncertainty cap the rally, but Q1 earnings +27% YoY and Iran ceasefire optimism provide a floor. Thursday's PCE print is the week's fulcrum — a hot number reopens the rate-hike trade and hits long-duration tech.
